Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and marked proteinuria and glucosuria, suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with low specific gravity, which may indicate tubular dysfunction. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended. No evidence of urinary tract infection.
